data.title: Empire State Realty Trust declares Q1 2026 dividend of $0.035 per share for Class A and B common stock holders.
data.description: Empire State Realty Trust, Inc. disclosed that its Board of Directors voted on a dividend of $0.035 per share for the initial quarter of 2026. This dividend will be distributed to shareholders of the Company's Class A and Class B common stock, as well as to holders of Empire State Realty OP, L.P.'s Series ES, Series 250, and Series 60 operating partnership units (NYSE Arca: ESBA, FISK, and OGCP respectively), along with the Series PR operating partnership unit.

data.title: Inventor from Akron creates VENDI SPORTS vending machine for sports equipment.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on February 20, 2026, an inventor from Akron, Ohio, shared their inspiration for creating VENDI SPORTS, a new vending machine concept aimed at providing convenience for purchasing sports equipment. The inventor saw the need for a practical method to acquire items like tennis balls, baseballs, basketballs, and water bottles at parks, fields, and sports facilities. The VENDI SPORTS machine offers a solution to this by offering these products in a convenient manner.

data.title: Celsior Technologies joins Jack Henry Fintech Integration Network, enabling integration with jXchange™.
data.description: Celsior Technologies, a leading provider of technology and workforce solutions for regulated industries worldwide, has partnered with FIN to integrate with jXchange™. This collaboration was announced on February 20, 2026. Celsior Technologies focuses on digital transformation and aims to enhance its services through this integration. FIN aims to support this partnership by providing the necessary tools for seamless integration. This move signifies a step forward for Celsior Technologies in expanding its capabilities and offerings.

data.title: Sensata reports Q4 earnings of 88 cents/share, beating estimates of 86 cents/share. Company sees Q1 EPS of 81-85 cents and sales of $917-937 million.
data.description: Sensata Technologies Holding plc (NYSE: ST) announced its Q4 earnings, surpassing expectations. The company reported earnings of 88 cents per share, exceeding the predicted 86 cents. Quarterly sales totaled $917.900 million, beating estimates of $910.199 million. For Q1, adjusted EPS is expected to range between 81 cents to 85 cents, compared to the market forecast of 83 cents. Anticipated sales for Q1 fall between $917.000 million to $937.000 million, while estimates were $924.999 million. The company emphasized achieving its objectives for the first year of its transformation journey, with improved margins, increased free cash flow, and a fortified balance sheet. For the full story, check Benzinga.com.

data.title: Mullen Group Ltd. declares monthly dividend of $0.07 per share for holders of record on Feb 28, payable on March 16.
data.description: In Okotoks, Alberta, on Feb. 20, 2026, Mullen Group Ltd. (TSX: MTL) board declared a monthly dividend of $0.07 per Common Share. The dividend is for holders of record of Common Shares by Feb. 28, 2026. The payment date is set for March 16, 2026.

data.title: Key contributor exiting Aave DAO; BGD Labs to depart after 4 years, cites protocol changes.
data.description: After being a member for four years, BGD Labs announced their departure from the DAO due to disagreements with changes within the organization. They expressed concerns over the liquidity protocol, leading them to adopt an "adversarial position" towards it.

data.title: Amazon stock rises after Supreme Court strikes down Trump's import tariffs, boosting e-commerce.
data.description: In a recent ruling, the Supreme Court invalidated the import tariffs set by President Trump, which could bring significant benefits to the e-commerce sector. Amazon's stock surged following the tariff decision. Additionally, e-commerce stocks experienced a notable increase in value after the Supreme Court's ruling against the tariffs.

data.title: Starfighters Space soars 110% this week, gains support from GE Aerospace for launch program.
data.description: Starfighters Space has joined forces with GE Aerospace to advance its launch program review to the next phase. The company's shares surged by a remarkable 110% over the course of this week. This collaboration with GE Aerospace has garnered significant support for the new aerospace stock. This exciting development was highlighted in a recent article from Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: Similarweb (SMWB) faces analyst downgrades post weak fourth-quarter sales of $72.758m, missing estimates. Customer base at 6,128. Sales outlook for FY26 at $305-$315m vs estimate $324.569m. Stock prices rise post news.
data.description: Similarweb Ltd. (NYSE: SMWB) saw its stock trading higher despite a recent decline following worse-than-expected fourth-quarter sales results. The company reported earnings of three cents per share, meeting the consensus estimate, but posted sales of $72.758 million, missing the analyst consensus of $76.442 million. As of December 31, 2025, Similarweb had 6,128 customers, up 11% Y/Y, with 454 customers having annual recurring revenue of $100,000 or more, a 12% Y/Y increase. Remaining performance obligations stood at $288.8 million, up 17% Y/Y. Similarweb forecasts FY26 sales of $305.000 million-$315.000 million, below the $324.569 million analyst estimate, and first-quarter sales of $72.000 million-$74.000 million, missing the street view of $77.812 million. The company received a downgrade from Citizens analyst Patrick Walravens from Market Outperform to Market Perform due to weak quarterly results.

data.title: Wayfair beats Q4 estimates with earnings of 85 cents per share on sales of $3.337 billion (+6.9% YoY), 21.3M active customers. CEO expects mid-single-digit revenue growth in Q1 2026.
data.description: Wayfair Inc. (NYSE: W) announced positive financial results for the fiscal fourth quarter of 2025. The company's adjusted earnings per share for the quarter were 85 cents, surpassing the analyst consensus of 66 cents. Quarterly sales reached $3.337 billion, marking a 6.9% increase from the previous year and exceeding the expected $3.301 billion. As of December 31, 2025, Wayfair had 21.3 million active customers, experiencing a slight 0.5% decrease year over year. Despite a contraction in the category for the final quarter of the year, CEO Niraj Shah highlighted the company's third consecutive quarter of new customer growth and healthy repeat orders. Looking ahead, Wayfair anticipates mid-single-digit revenue growth in the first quarter of 2026, with projected gross margins... (Remaining details can be found on Benzinga.com)

data.title: MARA Holdings acquires 64% of French HPC company Exaion, partnering with EDF and NJJ to grow European data centers.
data.description: MARA, a prominent Bitcoin miner, has successfully acquired a majority stake in Exaion. This strategic move involves partnering with EDF, a well-known energy company, and Xavier Niel's NJJ. The collaboration aims to enhance and expand European data center operations. This acquisition marks a significant development in the cryptocurrency sector, signifying MARA's commitment to growth and innovation.

data.title: Inventor from Summerville, S.C. develops new cat litter box called PRO-VENT to reduce odors/messes.
data.description: A resident of Summerville, South Carolina, mentioned that they were motivated to create a redesigned litter box to address odor and mess issues. This led to the invention of the PRO-VENT. The inventor stated that their creation provides a distinctive and improved space for cats to handle their basic needs. This invention was developed in 2026 in Pittsburgh.

data.title: S&P Global executives to present at Raymond James' 47th Annual Institutional Investors Conference on March 3, 2026. CEO and CFO to participate.
data.description: On March 3, 2026, Martina Cheung, President and CEO, and Eric Aboaf, CFO, of S&P Global (NYSE: SPGI), will attend Raymond James' 47th Annual Institutional Investors Conference. The session will be webcast for wider access and insight into the company's developments.

data.title: Bitcoin price unaffected by Supreme Court ruling on illegal tariffs, discussions of $150B refund.
data.description: The price of Bitcoin remained stable within a "descending trend" as the Supreme Court ruled that certain US trade tariffs were unlawful and subject to reimbursement.

data.title: Axelspace与Tri-Sat Constellation Co.签约合同，推进日本防卫省卫星星座项目，旨在实现“让太空变得触手可及”。
data.description: Tokyo - Leading microsatellite developer and operator, Axelspace Corporation ("Axelspace"), dedicated to making space more accessible, has announced a contract signing for optical image data acquisition with Tri-Sat Constellation Co., Ltd. and Mitsui Bussan Aerospace Co., Ltd., as part of advancing Japan's Ministry of Defense satellite constellation project. Tri-Sat Constellation Co., Ltd. is a Special Purpose Company (SPC) jointly established by Mitsubishi Electric Corporation, SKY Perfect JSAT Corporation, and Mitsui & Co., Ltd. The Ministry of Defense satellite constellation project involves Mitsubishi Electric Corporation, SKY Perfect JSAT Corporation, Mitsui & Co., Ltd., Synspective Inc., Institute for Q-shu Pioneers of Space, Inc., Mitsui Bussan Aerospace Co., Ltd., among others.

data.title: Deere & Co beat Q1 earnings expectations with $2.42 EPS vs $2.06 estimate. Revenue rose to $9.611 billion from $7.686 billion. CEO optimistic on recovery. Forecasted net income between $4.5 billion to $5.0 billion for fiscal 2026.
data.description: Deere & Company (NYSE: DE) exceeded expectations in its first quarter, with EPS at $2.42 surpassing the estimated $2.06. Global net sales and revenues rose by 13% to $9.611 billion, well above the $7.686 billion estimate. Chairman and CEO John May expressed optimism amidst challenges in the large agriculture sector, noting a rebound in demand for construction and small agriculture. Deere increased its fiscal 2026 net income guidance to between $4.5 billion and $5.0 billion. The company anticipates a 5-10% decline in Production & Precision Agriculture net sales, a 15% rise in Small Agriculture & Turf net sales, and a 15% increase in Construction & Forestry net sales.
